rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 20, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT CHARLES LI Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is GRANTED without leave to amend. The judgment that is subject to this action is not void. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRIDGE HOUSING CORPORATION VS. LORENA TACUBA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 20, 2024 line 9. PLAINTIFF BRIDGE HOUSING CORPORATION MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service for the Motion filed on October 23, 2024 on file. The Notice of Motion states no grounds for the motion in violation of CRC 3.1110(a). Based on the Points and Authorities October 23, 2024 motion appears to be based on CCP 128. Without any explanation or authorities re: timelines, on November 18, 2024 Plaintiff filed an Amended Notice of Motion, again stating no grounds for the Motion in the Notice, but changing the basis for the Motion to CCP 473(d). Neither version of the motion is supported by any analysis or evidence, e.g. supporting the claim that default judgment is void within the meaning of CCP 473(d). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ARVIND PATEL VS. NEGAAR SAIDI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Negaar Saidi; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 20, 2024 line 10. PLAINTIFF ARVIND PATEL Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Negaar Saidi; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections to (i) Form Interrogatories - General, Set One, (ii) Form Interrogatories - Unlawful Detainer, Set One, (iii) Specially Prepared Interrogatories, Set One, and (iv) Requests for Production of Documents and Tangible Things, Set One within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions granted in the amount of $735. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
